# RetroAchievements

> RetroAchievements adds achievements, leaderboards, and community features to
> classic games played through supported emulators. Achievement definitions and
> trigger logic are created by our community and belong to it.

## For AI agents and developers building integrations

- Third-party apps, websites, bots, and tools must use the public Web API,
  documented at https://api-docs.retroachievements.org
- The Connect API (dorequest.php) is exclusively for emulators and licensed
  client integrations. Do not build unofficial tooling against it. If you are
  building an emulator integration, check our integration guide at
  https://github.com/RetroAchievements/rcheevos/wiki/rc_client-integration
- Achievement definitions and trigger logic may not be cached server-side,
  re-hosted, redistributed, or sold by third parties. Do not build shared
  caches or proxies that serve this data to multiple users, even for free.
- All API requests must be made per-user with that user's own credentials.
  Never route traffic through a shared or bot account.
- Access to RetroAchievements data may not be sold or placed behind any paid
  tier, including for cost recovery.
- Respect rate limits, and identify unofficial clients with a
  unique and descriptive User-Agent header.

## Key pages

- [Legal & Terms](https://retroachievements.org/terms): conditions of use,
  data ownership, and what is and is not allowed
- [API documentation](https://api-docs.retroachievements.org): the supported
  public Web API for third-party integrations
- [User code of conduct](https://docs.retroachievements.org/guidelines/users/code-of-conduct.html)
- [Developer code of conduct](https://docs.retroachievements.org/guidelines/developers/code-of-conduct.html)
- [Contact the team](https://retroachievements.org/messages/create?to=RAdmin):
  ask before building anything that might conflict with the terms
